Relanto | Who we are English Spanish (Mexico) Get In TOUCh Business at the Convergence of AI Monetization and Innovation Amidst the upheaval of the pandemic, we found an opportunity. Founded in 2021, Relanto stepped in as AI began to revolutionize the business landscape. We identified the need for accelerated innovation to thrive in the AI era and set out to redefine business operations with AI leading every step of our journey. Since our inception, we've been in the fast lane of relentless innovation. In about 3 years, we've surged to over 1000-strong, partnering with Fortune 500 and Fortune 1000 leaders to redefine business norms. Our Core Values Our Mission To drive business excellence through monetizing AI, fostering innovation and delivering lasting value. Our Vision To be the global leader in advisory, consulting, and technology services, renowned for AI-ingrained innovations that propel businesses to thrive. Reshaping Industries with AI at the granular level , one solution at a time 1000+ Experts in the Global Team Trusted by 40+ of the Fortune Global 500 Simple. We put AI in the Driver's Seat. Harnessing the power of Granular-AI We harness the power of AI at a granular level, ensuring that every solution is infused with its transformative capabilities. We thrive on pushing boundaries, exploring the uncharted, and constantly challenging the status quo. Trust and reliability at the forefront Trust shapes how we move forward, while reliability anchors every step. Together, they create confidence that endures, even as we navigate change and complexity. [about_page]
Relanto | Who we are English Spanish (Mexico) Get In TOUCh Business at the Convergence of AI Monetization and Innovation Amidst the upheaval of the pandemic, we found an opportunity. Founded in 2021, Relanto stepped in as AI began to revolutionize the business landscape. We identified the need for accelerated innovation to thrive in the AI era and set out to redefine business operations with AI leading every step of our journey. Since our inception, we've been in the fast lane of relentless innovation. In about 3 years, we've surged to over 1000-strong, partnering with Fortune 500 and Fortune 1000 leaders to redefine business norms. Our Core Values Our Mission To drive business excellence through monetizing AI, fostering innovation and delivering lasting value. Our Vision To be the global leader in advisory, consulting, and technology services, renowned for AI-ingrained innovations that propel businesses to thrive. Reshaping Industries with AI at the granular level , one solution at a time 1000+ Experts in the Global Team Trusted by 40+ of the Fortune Global 500 Simple. We put AI in the Driver's Seat. Harnessing the power of Granular-AI We harness the power of AI at a granular level, ensuring that every solution is infused with its transformative capabilities. It's not just a tool for us; it's the beating heart of our innovation. Exploring the uncharted, challenging the status-quo But we don't stop there. We thrive on pushing boundaries, exploring the uncharted, and constantly challenging the status quo. Trust and reliability at the forefront Trust shapes how we move forward, while reliability anchors every step. Together, they create confidence that endures, even as we navigate change and complexity. Our People With over two decades of experience, spanning Service Industries and startups, our leadership team brings a wealth of expertise in areas including Product Engineering, Development, and Incubating new technologies and business units Rajan Gaur Chief Executive Officer Shankar Chowhan President Yeshwant Nayak Chief Operating Officer Venkatesh Kodangal Chief Strategy Officer Nadia Dessouki Chief Sales Officer T.P. Vincent Chief Technology Officer Prakash Hariharan Chief Planning Officer Ishita Majumdar Chief Data & AI Officer Vivek Kashyap VP - Delivery Santhi Kanumuri VP - Business Operations Our Presence With a presence in key locations worldwide, our offices are your gateway to AI-led global solutions. Relanto | Who we are English Spanish (Mexico) Get In TOUCh Business at the Convergence of AI Monetization and Innovation Amidst the upheaval of the pandemic, we found an opportunity. Founded in 2021, Relanto stepped in as AI began to revolutionize the business landscape. Since our inception, we've been in the fast lane of relentless innovation. In about 3 years, we've surged to over 1000-strong, partnering with Fortune 500 and Fortune 1000 leaders to redefine business norms. Our Core Values Our Mission To drive business excellence through monetizing AI, fostering innovation and delivering lasting value. Our Vision To be the global leader in advisory, consulting, and technology services, renowned for AI-ingrained innovations that propel businesses to thrive. Reshaping Industries with AI at the granular level , one solution at a time 1000+ Experts in the Global Team Trusted by 40+ of the Fortune Global 500 Simple. We put AI in the Driver's Seat. It's not just a tool for us; it's the beating heart of our innovation. Exploring the uncharted, challenging the status-quo But we don't stop there. Trust and reliability at the forefront Trust shapes how we move forward, while reliability anchors every step. [about_page]
About us Skip to content Company Overview We take inspiration from the legendary migratory bird – the Arctic Tern with an incredibly impressive circumpolar migratory lifestyle. At the center of our business model is our enduring philosophy of going the extra mile for our stakeholders. We embrace this journey, striving to remain the trusted partner to our clients delivering new-age solutions and services in the Automotive ecosystem – Connected Vehicle, Autonomous Driving. As technology continuously disrupts the norms, our clients can rely on us as a trusted partner through a successful technology transformation journey, innovation, and value creation. We have flagged our position as a reliable partner for our clients with the resources, the scale, and the know-how that our clients need. We are headquartered in Karlsruhe, Germany, with primary delivery centers in Bangalore, Chennai and Trivandrum, India. AUTOSAR Skip to content AUTOSAR Services Arctictern actively uses AUTOSAR in sophisticated software development that pursues the objective of creating and establishing an open and standardized system architecture for automotive electronic control units (ECUs). AUTOSAR (versions 3.x, 4.x) governed software development of smart, safe and secure vehicles focuses on standardization with the goal of reliability, scalability and maintainability by developing common specifications, architecture and methodology for dependable automotive systems. AUTOSAR compliant development is based on standard specifications (of a particular version). AUTOSAR standard BSW is highly configurable and reusable, hence is cost effective. Whereas Non-AUTOSAR based developments are based on legacy architecture, hardware specific and using proprietary tools, processes. We use Classic AUTOSAR architecture for development of real time safety critical automotive ECU systems such as: Powertrain Body Control Modules Instrument Clusters Gateways We enable the use of Adaptive AUTOSAR software standard for more powerful and more flexible E/E architectures in the vehicle software development. [about_page]
About Vectra: AI Driven Cybersecurity Company Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. About Vectra AI We’re redefining cyber attack resilience for AI enterprises From market-leading NDR to modern network resilience The current approach to network detection and response is no longer enough for today’s AI driven enterprises. Modern attacks move laterally across hybrid networks, identities, and multi-cloud environments at machine speed. Vectra AI builds on its market leading NDR foundation to deliver continuous observability, clear risk signal, and confident action across the modern network. For more than a decade, our researchers, data scientists, and engineers have focused on understanding real attacker behavior and turning it into reliable signal security teams can trust. 02:23 Real attacks. In real time. We find the attacks other tech can't, so you can stop them. 02:23 Vectra AI is named Highest in Execution and Vision in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for NDR Our story Since our founding in 2011, our mission has been simple, to help make the world a safer and fairer place. As enterprises have evolved into hybrid and AI driven environments where attacks move at machine speed, turning that mission into reality has required a fundamentally new approach to network detection and response. Over the years, we have built a team of security researchers, data scientists, and engineers focused on understanding how real attacks move across modern networks, identities, and cloud environments. This approach has helped more than 2,000 hybrid and multi cloud organizations continuously reduce exposure and stop advanced attacks before they become breaches. We help security teams turn the tables on attackers by applying artificial intelligence and machine learning to reveal exposure gaps, deliver clear and reliable attack signal with context, and enable confident action, while remaining absolutely obsessed with delivering a first class customer experience. Our founding belief still guides how we approach modern network detection and response today. From the desk of our founder Before my time at Vectra AI, a customer once told me about a sophisticated nation-state attack, which turned into a major breach, that affected them and several other companies. The customer asserted that the products my company had sold them could have alerted them of a critical attack. The assertion that enough signal to detect this breach was observable, and the advancements that AI and ML technologies were undergoing at the time convinced me to try to help protect organizations from such breaches. Thus, Vectra AI started in 2011 with the idea that AI and ML could be applied to the problem of finding and stopping sophisticated attacks. To accomplish that goal, we assembled a team of the industry’s most skilled data scientists, security researchers and software engineers to develop an innovative platform that delivers the most accurate attack signal. While we’re proud of our innovation we’ve gained along the way – the 35 security patents, 12 of which are referenced in MITRE D3FEND – our goal has always been to: Make the world a safer and fairer place. About Vectra AI We’re redefining cyber attack resilience for AI enterprises From market-leading NDR to modern network resilience The current approach to network detection and response is no longer enough for today’s AI driven enterprises. Modern attacks move laterally across hybrid networks, identities, and multi-cloud environments at machine speed. Vectra AI builds on its market leading NDR foundation to deliver continuous observability, clear risk signal, and confident action across the modern network. For more than a decade, our researchers, data scientists, and engineers have focused on understanding real attacker behavior and turning it into reliable signal security teams can trust. 02:23 Real attacks. In real time. We find the attacks other tech can't, so you can stop them. 02:23 Vectra AI is named Highest in Execution and Vision in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for NDR Our story Since our founding in 2011, our mission has been simple, to help make the world a safer and fairer place. As enterprises have evolved into hybrid and AI driven environments where attacks move at machine speed, turning that mission into reality has required a fundamentally new approach to network detection and response. Over the years, we have built a team of security researchers, data scientists, and engineers focused on understanding how real attacks move across modern networks, identities, and cloud environments. This approach has helped more than 2,000 hybrid and multi cloud organizations continuously reduce exposure and stop advanced attacks before they become breaches. We help security teams turn the tables on attackers by applying artificial intelligence and machine learning to reveal exposure gaps, deliver clear and reliable attack signal with context, and enable confident action, while remaining absolutely obsessed with delivering a first class customer experience. Our founding belief still guides how we approach modern network detection and response today. From the desk of our founder Before my time at Vectra AI, a customer once told me about a sophisticated nation-state attack, which turned into a major breach, that affected them and several other companies. The customer asserted that the products my company had sold them could have alerted them of a critical attack. The assertion that enough signal to detect this breach was observable, and the advancements that AI and ML technologies were undergoing at the time convinced me to try to help protect organizations from such breaches. Thus, Vectra AI started in 2011 with the idea that AI and ML could be applied to the problem of finding and stopping sophisticated attacks. To accomplish that goal, we assembled a team of the industry’s most skilled data scientists, security researchers and software engineers to develop an innovative platform that delivers the most accurate attack signal. While we’re proud of our innovation we’ve gained along the way – the 35 security patents, 12 of which are referenced in MITRE D3FEND – our goal has always been to: Make the world a safer and fairer place. From day 1, we've been striving to keep people, their data, and their way of life safe. Build a culture we could be proud of. Companies come and go, but the legacy of Vectra AI will be one of integrity and compassion. Leverage AI to its full potential to do good. Because when you arm human intelligence, great things can happen. We wouldn’t be here today if it weren’t for the partnership with our amazing customers. About Vectra: AI Driven Cybersecurity Company Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. About Vectra AI We’re redefining cyber attack resilience for AI enterprises From market-leading NDR to modern network resilience The current approach to network detection and response is no longer enough for today’s AI driven enterprises. Modern attacks move laterally across hybrid networks, identities, and multi-cloud environments at machine speed. Vectra AI builds on its market leading NDR foundation to deliver continuous observability, clear risk signal, and confident action across the modern network. For more than a decade, our researchers, data scientists, and engineers have focused on understanding real attacker behavior and turning it into reliable signal security teams can trust. 02:23 Real attacks. In real time. We find the attacks other tech can't, so you can stop them. 02:23 Vectra AI is named Highest in Execution and Vision in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for NDR Our story Since our founding in 2011, our mission has been simple, to help make the world a safer and fairer place. As enterprises have evolved into hybrid and AI driven environments where attacks move at machine speed, turning that mission into reality has required a fundamentally new approach to network detection and response. Over the years, we have built a team of security researchers, data scientists, and engineers focused on understanding how real attacks move across modern networks, identities, and cloud environments. This approach has helped more than 2,000 hybrid and multi cloud organizations continuously reduce exposure and stop advanced attacks before they become breaches. We help security teams turn the tables on attackers by applying artificial intelligence and machine learning to reveal exposure gaps, deliver clear and reliable attack signal with context, and enable confident action, while remaining absolutely obsessed with delivering a first class customer experience. Our founding belief still guides how we approach modern network detection and response today. From the desk of our founder Before my time at Vectra AI, a customer once told me about a sophisticated nation-state attack, which turned into a major breach, that affected them and several other companies. The customer asserted that the products my company had sold them could have alerted them of a critical attack. The assertion that enough signal to detect this breach was observable, and the advancements that AI and ML technologies were undergoing at the time convinced me to try to help protect organizations from such breaches. Thus, Vectra AI started in 2011 with the idea that AI and ML could be applied to the problem of finding and stopping sophisticated attacks. To accomplish that goal, we assembled a team of the industry’s most skilled data scientists, security researchers and software engineers to develop an innovative platform that delivers the most accurate attack signal. While we’re proud of our innovation we’ve gained along the way – the 35 security patents, 12 of which are referenced in MITRE D3FEND – our goal has always been to: Make the world a safer and fairer place. From day 1, we've been striving to keep people, their data, and their way of life safe. Build a culture we could be proud of. Companies come and go, but the legacy of Vectra AI will be one of integrity and compassion. Leverage AI to its full potential to do good. Because when you arm human intelligence, great things can happen. We wouldn’t be here today if it weren’t for the partnership with our amazing customers. It's your passion and commitment to making the world safer that keeps us innovating. I'm incredibly grateful for the opportunity to partner with you. Thank you! Hitesh Sheth Founder and CEO Our values Customers first For over a decade, we’ve been obsessed with creating what security analysts and security team leaders have repeatedly told us is most critical: creating the most accurate attack signal. We have teams of security researchers, data scientists and software engineers who are laser focused on perfecting our signal, improving it every day. Our company is committed to providing the best attack signal on the planet, providing an easy-to-use platform and providing white-glove, proactive and responsive service along with shared accountability to make the lives of our customers easier. Integrity Integrity is our foundation. It drives every decision we make and every action we take. We believe in doing what is right, even when no one is watching. We prioritize honesty, transparency and fairness, earning your trust every step of the way. Teamwork We value teamwork that is focused, efficient and drama-free. We respect and challenge each other to do the best work we can. The collaborative culture we've cultivated means we can accomplish extraordinary feats. Our customers Security team analysts get an average of 4,484 alerts a day Vectra AI exists to help analysts, leaders, and CISOs reduce exposure, cut noise, and stop real attacks using modern network detection and response. But while other vendors flood enterprises with pointless alerts caused by signatures and basic anomaly detection, we expertly employ AI and ML to deliver the most reliable attack signal on the planet. It's the difference between managing pointless detections and investigating and responding to real attacks. And it's the reason we have over 95% customer retention. “Our alert volume has been reduced by 90% since Vectra AI’s ML assesses more features and context in the models, which leads to more accurate detections.” Kevin Kennedy Senior Vice President, Cybersecurity, Blackstone Go in-depth See all customers stories Our people Our culture Great Bay Area Top Workplace, 2023 Top Workplace Top Worlplaces for Work-Life Flexibility 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplaces Technology 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplaces Innovation 2023 Top Workplace Greater Austin Top Workplace, 2022 Top Workplace Top Workplaces for Great Leadership 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplace for Compensation and Benefits, 2023 Top Workplace Join us Our leadership Backed by deep expertise in AI security, our leaders don’t just guide Vectra AI forward, they shape the future of the cybersecurity industry. Leadership Fast facts 2011 Over 10 years of expertise 580+ Employees and growing 35 AI security patents—more than any other cybersecurity company 12 MITRE D3FEND references—also more than any other company 7M+ Hosts monitored 113 Countries we’re operating in—more than half the countries in the world 468 Transacting Partners 95%+ Customer Retention Ever wish modern network security could be more simple? Hybrid and multi-cloud enterprises are drowning in a sea of more. More attack surface. More blind spots. More noise. Vectra AI stops this vicious cycle by providing the only “more” security teams actually need — more signal clarity. Vectra AI breaks this cycle by delivering the only more security teams actually need: clear signal with context. Our advanced AI signal helps teams understand where exposure exists, focus on what matters most, and stop real attacks with confidence. Explore our AI The Platform Protecting modern networks from modern attacks with modern network detection and response Our platform delivers modern network detection and response through unified attack surface coverage, clear signal with context, and real time control, helping security teams reduce exposure and stop attacks early. About Vectra: AI Driven Cybersecurity Company Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. About Vectra AI We’re redefining cyber attack resilience for AI enterprises From market-leading NDR to modern network resilience The current approach to network detection and response is no longer enough for today’s AI driven enterprises. Modern attacks move laterally across hybrid networks, identities, and multi-cloud environments at machine speed. Vectra AI builds on its market leading NDR foundation to deliver continuous observability, clear risk signal, and confident action across the modern network. For more than a decade, our researchers, data scientists, and engineers have focused on understanding real attacker behavior and turning it into reliable signal security teams can trust. 02:23 Real attacks. In real time. We find the attacks other tech can't, so you can stop them. 02:23 Vectra AI is named Highest in Execution and Vision in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for NDR Our story Since our founding in 2011, our mission has been simple, to help make the world a safer and fairer place. As enterprises have evolved into hybrid and AI driven environments where attacks move at machine speed, turning that mission into reality has required a fundamentally new approach to network detection and response. Over the years, we have built a team of security researchers, data scientists, and engineers focused on understanding how real attacks move across modern networks, identities, and cloud environments. This approach has helped more than 2,000 hybrid and multi cloud organizations continuously reduce exposure and stop advanced attacks before they become breaches. We help security teams turn the tables on attackers by applying artificial intelligence and machine learning to reveal exposure gaps, deliver clear and reliable attack signal with context, and enable confident action, while remaining absolutely obsessed with delivering a first class customer experience. Our founding belief still guides how we approach modern network detection and response today. From the desk of our founder Before my time at Vectra AI, a customer once told me about a sophisticated nation-state attack, which turned into a major breach, that affected them and several other companies. The customer asserted that the products my company had sold them could have alerted them of a critical attack. The assertion that enough signal to detect this breach was observable, and the advancements that AI and ML technologies were undergoing at the time convinced me to try to help protect organizations from such breaches. Thus, Vectra AI started in 2011 with the idea that AI and ML could be applied to the problem of finding and stopping sophisticated attacks. To accomplish that goal, we assembled a team of the industry’s most skilled data scientists, security researchers and software engineers to develop an innovative platform that delivers the most accurate attack signal. While we’re proud of our innovation we’ve gained along the way – the 35 security patents, 12 of which are referenced in MITRE D3FEND – our goal has always been to: Make the world a safer and fairer place. From day 1, we've been striving to keep people, their data, and their way of life safe. Build a culture we could be proud of. Companies come and go, but the legacy of Vectra AI will be one of integrity and compassion. Leverage AI to its full potential to do good. Because when you arm human intelligence, great things can happen. We wouldn’t be here today if it weren’t for the partnership with our amazing customers. It's your passion and commitment to making the world safer that keeps us innovating. I'm incredibly grateful for the opportunity to partner with you. Thank you! Hitesh Sheth Founder and CEO Our values Customers first For over a decade, we’ve been obsessed with creating what security analysts and security team leaders have repeatedly told us is most critical: creating the most accurate attack signal. We have teams of security researchers, data scientists and software engineers who are laser focused on perfecting our signal, improving it every day. Our company is committed to providing the best attack signal on the planet, providing an easy-to-use platform and providing white-glove, proactive and responsive service along with shared accountability to make the lives of our customers easier. Integrity Integrity is our foundation. It drives every decision we make and every action we take. We believe in doing what is right, even when no one is watching. We prioritize honesty, transparency and fairness, earning your trust every step of the way. Teamwork We value teamwork that is focused, efficient and drama-free. We respect and challenge each other to do the best work we can. The collaborative culture we've cultivated means we can accomplish extraordinary feats. Our customers Security team analysts get an average of 4,484 alerts a day Vectra AI exists to help analysts, leaders, and CISOs reduce exposure, cut noise, and stop real attacks using modern network detection and response. But while other vendors flood enterprises with pointless alerts caused by signatures and basic anomaly detection, we expertly employ AI and ML to deliver the most reliable attack signal on the planet. It's the difference between managing pointless detections and investigating and responding to real attacks. And it's the reason we have over 95% customer retention. “Our alert volume has been reduced by 90% since Vectra AI’s ML assesses more features and context in the models, which leads to more accurate detections.” Kevin Kennedy Senior Vice President, Cybersecurity, Blackstone Go in-depth See all customers stories Our people Our culture Great Bay Area Top Workplace, 2023 Top Workplace Top Worlplaces for Work-Life Flexibility 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplaces Technology 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplaces Innovation 2023 Top Workplace Greater Austin Top Workplace, 2022 Top Workplace Top Workplaces for Great Leadership 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplace for Compensation and Benefits, 2023 Top Workplace Join us Our leadership Backed by deep expertise in AI security, our leaders don’t just guide Vectra AI forward, they shape the future of the cybersecurity industry. Leadership Fast facts 2011 Over 10 years of expertise 580+ Employees and growing 35 AI security patents—more than any other cybersecurity company 12 MITRE D3FEND references—also more than any other company 7M+ Hosts monitored 113 Countries we’re operating in—more than half the countries in the world 468 Transacting Partners 95%+ Customer Retention Ever wish modern network security could be more simple? Hybrid and multi-cloud enterprises are drowning in a sea of more. More attack surface. More blind spots. More noise. Vectra AI stops this vicious cycle by providing the only “more” security teams actually need — more signal clarity. Vectra AI breaks this cycle by delivering the only more security teams actually need: clear signal with context. Our advanced AI signal helps teams understand where exposure exists, focus on what matters most, and stop real attacks with confidence. About Vectra: AI Driven Cybersecurity Company Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. About Vectra AI We’re redefining cyber attack resilience for AI enterprises From market-leading NDR to modern network resilience The current approach to network detection and response is no longer enough for today’s AI driven enterprises. Modern attacks move laterally across hybrid networks, identities, and multi-cloud environments at machine speed. Vectra AI builds on its market leading NDR foundation to deliver continuous observability, clear risk signal, and confident action across the modern network. For more than a decade, our researchers, data scientists, and engineers have focused on understanding real attacker behavior and turning it into reliable signal security teams can trust. 02:23 Real attacks. In real time. We find the attacks other tech can't, so you can stop them. 02:23 Vectra AI is named Highest in Execution and Vision in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for NDR Our story Since our founding in 2011, our mission has been simple, to help make the world a safer and fairer place. As enterprises have evolved into hybrid and AI driven environments where attacks move at machine speed, turning that mission into reality has required a fundamentally new approach to network detection and response. Over the years, we have built a team of security researchers, data scientists, and engineers focused on understanding how real attacks move across modern networks, identities, and cloud environments. This approach has helped more than 2,000 hybrid and multi cloud organizations continuously reduce exposure and stop advanced attacks before they become breaches. We help security teams turn the tables on attackers by applying artificial intelligence and machine learning to reveal exposure gaps, deliver clear and reliable attack signal with context, and enable confident action, while remaining absolutely obsessed with delivering a first class customer experience. Our founding belief still guides how we approach modern network detection and response today. From the desk of our founder Before my time at Vectra AI, a customer once told me about a sophisticated nation-state attack, which turned into a major breach, that affected them and several other companies. The customer asserted that the products my company had sold them could have alerted them of a critical attack. The assertion that enough signal to detect this breach was observable, and the advancements that AI and ML technologies were undergoing at the time convinced me to try to help protect organizations from such breaches. Thus, Vectra AI started in 2011 with the idea that AI and ML could be applied to the problem of finding and stopping sophisticated attacks. To accomplish that goal, we assembled a team of the industry’s most skilled data scientists, security researchers and software engineers to develop an innovative platform that delivers the most accurate attack signal. While we’re proud of our innovation we’ve gained along the way – the 35 security patents, 12 of which are referenced in MITRE D3FEND – our goal has always been to: Make the world a safer and fairer place. From day 1, we've been striving to keep people, their data, and their way of life safe. Build a culture we could be proud of. Companies come and go, but the legacy of Vectra AI will be one of integrity and compassion. Leverage AI to its full potential to do good. Because when you arm human intelligence, great things can happen. We wouldn’t be here today if it weren’t for the partnership with our amazing customers. It's your passion and commitment to making the world safer that keeps us innovating. I'm incredibly grateful for the opportunity to partner with you. Thank you! Hitesh Sheth Founder and CEO Our values Customers first For over a decade, we’ve been obsessed with creating what security analysts and security team leaders have repeatedly told us is most critical: creating the most accurate attack signal. We have teams of security researchers, data scientists and software engineers who are laser focused on perfecting our signal, improving it every day. Our company is committed to providing the best attack signal on the planet, providing an easy-to-use platform and providing white-glove, proactive and responsive service along with shared accountability to make the lives of our customers easier. Integrity Integrity is our foundation. It drives every decision we make and every action we take. We believe in doing what is right, even when no one is watching. We prioritize honesty, transparency and fairness, earning your trust every step of the way. Teamwork We value teamwork that is focused, efficient and drama-free. We respect and challenge each other to do the best work we can. The collaborative culture we've cultivated means we can accomplish extraordinary feats. Our customers Security team analysts get an average of 4,484 alerts a day Vectra AI exists to help analysts, leaders, and CISOs reduce exposure, cut noise, and stop real attacks using modern network detection and response. But while other vendors flood enterprises with pointless alerts caused by signatures and basic anomaly detection, we expertly employ AI and ML to deliver the most reliable attack signal on the planet. It's the difference between managing pointless detections and investigating and responding to real attacks. And it's the reason we have over 95% customer retention. “Our alert volume has been reduced by 90% since Vectra AI’s ML assesses more features and context in the models, which leads to more accurate detections.” Kevin Kennedy Senior Vice President, Cybersecurity, Blackstone Go in-depth See all customers stories Our people Our culture Great Bay Area Top Workplace, 2023 Top Workplace Top Worlplaces for Work-Life Flexibility 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplaces Technology 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplaces Innovation 2023 Top Workplace Greater Austin Top Workplace, 2022 Top Workplace Top Workplaces for Great Leadership 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplace for Compensation and Benefits, 2023 Top Workplace Join us Our leadership Backed by deep expertise in AI security, our leaders don’t just guide Vectra AI forward, they shape the future of the cybersecurity industry. Leadership Fast facts 2011 Over 10 years of expertise 580+ Employees and growing 35 AI security patents—more than any other cybersecurity company 12 MITRE D3FEND references—also more than any other company 7M+ Hosts monitored 113 Countries we’re operating in—more than half the countries in the world 468 Transacting Partners 95%+ Customer Retention Ever wish modern network security could be more simple? Hybrid and multi-cloud enterprises are drowning in a sea of more. More attack surface. More blind spots. More noise. Vectra AI stops this vicious cycle by providing the only “more” security teams actually need — more signal clarity. Vectra AI breaks this cycle by delivering the only more security teams actually need: clear signal with context. Our advanced AI signal helps teams understand where exposure exists, focus on what matters most, and stop real attacks with confidence. Explore our AI The Platform Protecting modern networks from modern attacks with modern network detection and response Our platform delivers modern network detection and response through unified attack surface coverage, clear signal with context, and real time control, helping security teams reduce exposure and stop attacks early. About Vectra: AI Driven Cybersecurity Company Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. About Vectra AI We’re redefining cyber attack resilience for AI enterprises From market-leading NDR to modern network resilience The current approach to network detection and response is no longer enough for today’s AI driven enterprises. Modern attacks move laterally across hybrid networks, identities, and multi-cloud environments at machine speed. Vectra AI builds on its market leading NDR foundation to deliver continuous observability, clear risk signal, and confident action across the modern network. For more than a decade, our researchers, data scientists, and engineers have focused on understanding real attacker behavior and turning it into reliable signal security teams can trust. 02:23 Real attacks. In real time. We find the attacks other tech can't, so you can stop them. 02:23 Vectra AI is named Highest in Execution and Vision in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for NDR Our story Since our founding in 2011, our mission has been simple, to help make the world a safer and fairer place. As enterprises have evolved into hybrid and AI driven environments where attacks move at machine speed, turning that mission into reality has required a fundamentally new approach to network detection and response. Over the years, we have built a team of security researchers, data scientists, and engineers focused on understanding how real attacks move across modern networks, identities, and cloud environments. This approach has helped more than 2,000 hybrid and multi cloud organizations continuously reduce exposure and stop advanced attacks before they become breaches. We help security teams turn the tables on attackers by applying artificial intelligence and machine learning to reveal exposure gaps, deliver clear and reliable attack signal with context, and enable confident action, while remaining absolutely obsessed with delivering a first class customer experience. Our founding belief still guides how we approach modern network detection and response today. From the desk of our founder Before my time at Vectra AI, a customer once told me about a sophisticated nation-state attack, which turned into a major breach, that affected them and several other companies. The customer asserted that the products my company had sold them could have alerted them of a critical attack. The assertion that enough signal to detect this breach was observable, and the advancements that AI and ML technologies were undergoing at the time convinced me to try to help protect organizations from such breaches. Thus, Vectra AI started in 2011 with the idea that AI and ML could be applied to the problem of finding and stopping sophisticated attacks. To accomplish that goal, we assembled a team of the industry’s most skilled data scientists, security researchers and software engineers to develop an innovative platform that delivers the most accurate attack signal. While we’re proud of our innovation we’ve gained along the way – the 35 security patents, 12 of which are referenced in MITRE D3FEND – our goal has always been to: Make the world a safer and fairer place. From day 1, we've been striving to keep people, their data, and their way of life safe. Build a culture we could be proud of. Companies come and go, but the legacy of Vectra AI will be one of integrity and compassion. Leverage AI to its full potential to do good. Because when you arm human intelligence, great things can happen. We wouldn’t be here today if it weren’t for the partnership with our amazing customers. It's your passion and commitment to making the world safer that keeps us innovating. I'm incredibly grateful for the opportunity to partner with you. Thank you! Hitesh Sheth Founder and CEO Our values Customers first For over a decade, we’ve been obsessed with creating what security analysts and security team leaders have repeatedly told us is most critical: creating the most accurate attack signal. We have teams of security researchers, data scientists and software engineers who are laser focused on perfecting our signal, improving it every day. Our company is committed to providing the best attack signal on the planet, providing an easy-to-use platform and providing white-glove, proactive and responsive service along with shared accountability to make the lives of our customers easier. Integrity Integrity is our foundation. It drives every decision we make and every action we take. We believe in doing what is right, even when no one is watching. We prioritize honesty, transparency and fairness, earning your trust every step of the way. Teamwork We value teamwork that is focused, efficient and drama-free. We respect and challenge each other to do the best work we can. The collaborative culture we've cultivated means we can accomplish extraordinary feats. Our customers Security team analysts get an average of 4,484 alerts a day Vectra AI exists to help analysts, leaders, and CISOs reduce exposure, cut noise, and stop real attacks using modern network detection and response. But while other vendors flood enterprises with pointless alerts caused by signatures and basic anomaly detection, we expertly employ AI and ML to deliver the most reliable attack signal on the planet. It's the difference between managing pointless detections and investigating and responding to real attacks. And it's the reason we have over 95% customer retention. “Our alert volume has been reduced by 90% since Vectra AI’s ML assesses more features and context in the models, which leads to more accurate detections.” Kevin Kennedy Senior Vice President, Cybersecurity, Blackstone Go in-depth See all customers stories Our people Our culture Great Bay Area Top Workplace, 2023 Top Workplace Top Worlplaces for Work-Life Flexibility 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplaces Technology 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplaces Innovation 2023 Top Workplace Greater Austin Top Workplace, 2022 Top Workplace Top Workplaces for Great Leadership 2023 Top Workplace Top Workplace for Compensation and Benefits, 2023 Top Workplace Join us Our leadership Backed by deep expertise in AI security, our leaders don’t just guide Vectra AI forward, they shape the future of the cybersecurity industry. Hybrid and multi-cloud enterprises are drowning in a sea of more. More attack surface. More blind spots. More noise. Vectra AI stops this vicious cycle by providing the only “more” security teams actually need — more signal clarity. Vectra AI breaks this cycle by delivering the only more security teams actually need: clear signal with context. Our advanced AI signal helps teams understand where exposure exists, focus on what matters most, and stop real attacks with confidence. Meet our leadership team See All Nikesh Arora Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Read more BJ Jenkins President Read more Karim Temsamani President, Next Generation Security Read more Lee Klarich Chief Product and Technology Officer Read more Nir Zuk Founder - Emeritus Read more Dipak Golechha Chief Financial Officer Read more Helmut Reisinger CEO for EMEA Read more Bruce R. Byrd Executive Vice President and General Counsel Read more Kelly Waldher Chief Marketing Officer Read more Meerah Rajavel Chief Information Officer Read more Danielle Gonzalez Chief People Officer Read more Nikesh Arora Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Nikesh Arora is chairman and CEO of Palo Alto Networks, the world’s leading cybersecurity company. During his tenure of seven years, he has led the company through a major transformation to become the global leader in AI and cybersecurity, and the security partner of choice for enterprise organizations and governments around the world. Before joining Palo Alto Networks in 2018, Mr. Arora served as president and chief operating officer of SoftBank Group Corp. Prior to that, Mr. Arora spent ten years at Google, Inc. as a senior executive, where he was senior vice president and chief business officer, president of global sales operations and business development, and president of Europe, the Middle East and Africa. His career also includes serving as chief marketing officer for the T-Mobile International Division of Deutsche Telekom AG, and chief executive officer and founder of T-Motion PLC, which merged with T-Mobile International in 2002. Nikesh Arora serves on the board of Uber Technologies, Inc., and as a non-executive director of Compagnie Financière Richemont S.A., a public Switzerland-based luxury goods holding company. Previously, he served on the boards of SoftBank Group Corp., Airtel, Aviva, Sprint Corp., Colgate-Palmolive Inc., Tipping Point and Yahoo! Japan, among others. He holds an M.B.A. in business administration from Northeastern University, an M.S. in finance from Boston College, and a B. Tech. in electrical engineering from the Institute of Technology at Banaras Hindu University. He has received numerous awards for leadership and influence shaping the culture of Palo Alto Networks including Newsweek's Most Loved Workplaces (2023, 2022, 2021), CRN’s Top 100 Executives “Most influential”, Comparably’s Annual list of Best CEOs for Women, with a score of 100 on the Disability Equality Index (2023, 2022), and HRC Best Places for LGBTQ+ Equality (2022). close BJ Jenkins President BJ joined Palo Alto Networks in August 2021. As president, BJ oversees the company’s go-to-market functions, including sales, support, consulting, business development and operations, and partnerships. Previously, BJ served as president and CEO of Barracuda Networks, leading the company’s IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, as well as the strategic acquisitions of SignNow, Intronis, Sonian and PhishLine. Prior to joining Barracuda, BJ held multiple business unit and sales and marketing leadership roles at EMC, including president of EMC’s Backup Recovery Systems (BRS) Division. BJ earned an engineering degree from the University of Illinois and an M.B.A. from Harvard Business School. close Karim Temsamani President, Next Generation Security Karim Temsamani joined Palo Alto Networks in September 2024. As President, Next Generation Security, Karim leads GTM programs and global sales for the company's cloud security, security operations and network security platforms. Karim has spent his career building and scaling global businesses. Previously, he served as CEO of Cardlytics, a digital advertising platform widely used in the banking industry. Prior to Cardlytics, Karim led product and engineering for Stripe's banking and financial products. He also spent 12 years at Google, where he oversaw sales and operations across the AsiaPacific region and established Google's successful mobile advertising business. Karim began his career in the media and publishing industries and earned a degree in International Affairs at the European Business School Paris. close Lee Klarich Chief Product and Technology Officer Since early product inception in 2006, Lee Klarich has served as the head of product management at Palo Alto Networks, overseeing the product strategy and roadmap and playing a key role in delivering our Next-Generation Security Platform. In August 2025, he joined the Palo Alto Networks Board of Directors and became chief product and technology officer with responsibility for driving the company's technology vision and leading both the engineering and product organizations for the company. Prior to Palo Alto Networks, he was the director of product management for Juniper Networks, where he was responsible for firewall/VPN platforms and software. He joined Juniper Networks through the NetScreen Technologies acquisition, where he managed the same product line. Previously, he held various positions at Excite@Home and Packard Bell-NEC. He holds a bachelor's degree in engineering from Cornell University. close Nir Zuk Founder - Emeritus Nir Zuk brings a wealth of network security expertise and industry experience to Palo Alto Networks. Prior to co-founding Palo Alto Networks, Nir was CTO at NetScreen Technologies, which was acquired by Juniper Networks in 2004. Prior to NetScreen, Nir was co-founder and CTO at OneSecure, a pioneer in intrusion prevention and detection appliances. Nir was also a principal engineer at Check Point Software Technologies, and was one of the developers of stateful inspection technology. close Dipak Golechha Chief Financial Officer Dipak Golechha joined Palo Alto Networks in 2020 and serves as chief financial officer. A seasoned finance leader, prior to Palo Alto Networks, Golechha held a variety of global leadership and executive roles around the globe. During 18 years at Procter & Gamble, he served as global divisional CFO and chief operating officer. Following that, he was the CFO for Chobani and the CFO at The Nature’s Bounty Company, a $3 billion+ CPG company focused on health and wellness. Golechha also served four years as CEO of Excelligence Learning Corp, a tech-enabled education company. Golechha previously served as a board member of Holland & Barrett International, Dr Organic, and Procter & Gamble Financial Services. He holds both bachelor’s and master’s degrees in economics from St. John’s College, Cambridge University, UK. close Helmut Reisinger CEO for EMEA As CEO for Europe, the Middle East and Africa (EMEA), Helmut is responsible for all go-to-market functions across EMEA. Prior to joining Palo Alto Networks in 2022, Helmut was CEO of Orange Business Services (OBS), leading its global organization of 28,000 employees and supporting the digital transformation of enterprise customers around the world. Prior to OBS, Helmut held leadership positions across Europe at Avaya Inc., NextiraOne Germany and Alcatel Austria. Helmut holds a Ph.D. from Vienna University for Economics and Business (WU), and speaks English, French, German and Spanish. close Bruce R. Byrd Executive Vice President and General Counsel Bruce is responsible, globally, for legal affairs, policy and government affairs, corporate governance, compliance, and risk and resilience at the world’s leading cybersecurity company. He joined the company in January 2021. Before joining Palo Alto Networks, Bruce was the Chief Legal Officer of AT&T Communications, responsible for legal affairs at AT&T’s largest operating company. Prior to AT&T, Bruce was a partner at Dickinson Wright PLLC, where he maintained an active commercial litigation, regulatory and class action defense practice. Bruce is committed to community service. He is on the Board of Directors for the International Institute for Conflict Prevention and Resolution, and chairs the Bay Area Heart Walk for the American Heart Association. Bruce graduated from the University of Michigan with a degree in economics and received his J.D., cum laude, from the University of Michigan Law School. close Kelly Waldher Chief Marketing Officer Kelly Waldher is Chief Marketing Officer for Palo Alto Networks. He is responsible for the transformation and scale of the company's go-to-market initiatives, establishing Palo Alto Networks as the foremost authority in AI and cybersecurity. By leading the company’s global marketing efforts, Kelly aims to position Palo Alto Networks as the go-to platform for cutting-edge, AI-powered security solutions. Kelly is a tenured marketing executive with a diverse background spanning top tech companies. Prior to joining Palo Alto Networks, he served as the CMO at Zendesk, where he led marketing, communications and sales development teams and was the General Manager of the online business segment. As the Vice President for Google Workspace, he oversaw global marketing functions, including brand management, product marketing, demand generation, growth strategies

Meet our leadership team See All Nikesh Arora Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Read more BJ Jenkins President Read more Karim Temsamani President, Next Generation Security Read more Lee Klarich Chief Product and Technology Officer Read more Nir Zuk Founder - Emeritus Read more Dipak Golechha Chief Financial Officer Read more Helmut Reisinger CEO for EMEA Read more Bruce R. Byrd Executive Vice President and General Counsel Read more Kelly Waldher Chief Marketing Officer Read more Meerah Rajavel Chief Information Officer Read more Danielle Gonzalez Chief People Officer Read more Nikesh Arora Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Nikesh Arora is chairman and CEO of Palo Alto Networks, the world’s leading cybersecurity company. During his tenure of seven years, he has led the company through a major transformation to become the global leader in AI and cybersecurity, and the security partner of choice for enterprise organizations and governments around the world. Before joining Palo Alto Networks in 2018, Mr. Arora served as president and chief operating officer of SoftBank Group Corp. Prior to that, Mr. Arora spent ten years at Google, Inc. as a senior executive, where he was senior vice president and chief business officer, president of global sales operations and business development, and president of Europe, the Middle East and Africa. His career also includes serving as chief marketing officer for the T-Mobile International Division of Deutsche Telekom AG, and chief executive officer and founder of T-Motion PLC, which merged with T-Mobile International in 2002. Nikesh Arora serves on the board of Uber Technologies, Inc., and as a non-executive director of Compagnie Financière Richemont S.A., a public Switzerland-based luxury goods holding company. Previously, he served on the boards of SoftBank Group Corp., Airtel, Aviva, Sprint Corp., Colgate-Palmolive Inc., Tipping Point and Yahoo! Japan, among others. He holds an M.B.A. in business administration from Northeastern University, an M.S. in finance from Boston College, and a B. Tech. in electrical engineering from the Institute of Technology at Banaras Hindu University. He has received numerous awards for leadership and influence shaping the culture of Palo Alto Networks including Newsweek's Most Loved Workplaces (2023, 2022, 2021), CRN’s Top 100 Executives “Most influential”, Comparably’s Annual list of Best CEOs for Women, with a score of 100 on the Disability Equality Index (2023, 2022), and HRC Best Places for LGBTQ+ Equality (2022). close BJ Jenkins President BJ joined Palo Alto Networks in August 2021. As president, BJ oversees the company’s go-to-market functions, including sales, support, consulting, business development and operations, and partnerships. Previously, BJ served as president and CEO of Barracuda Networks, leading the company’s IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, as well as the strategic acquisitions of SignNow, Intronis, Sonian and PhishLine. Prior to joining Barracuda, BJ held multiple business unit and sales and marketing leadership roles at EMC, including president of EMC’s Backup Recovery Systems (BRS) Division. BJ earned an engineering degree from the University of Illinois and an M.B.A. from Harvard Business School. close Karim Temsamani President, Next Generation Security Karim Temsamani joined Palo Alto Networks in September 2024. As President, Next Generation Security, Karim leads GTM programs and global sales for the company's cloud security, security operations and network security platforms. Karim has spent his career building and scaling global businesses. Previously, he served as CEO of Cardlytics, a digital advertising platform widely used in the banking industry. Prior to Cardlytics, Karim led product and engineering for Stripe's banking and financial products. He also spent 12 years at Google, where he oversaw sales and operations across the AsiaPacific region and established Google's successful mobile advertising business. Karim began his career in the media and publishing industries and earned a degree in International Affairs at the European Business School Paris. close Lee Klarich Chief Product and Technology Officer Since early product inception in 2006, Lee Klarich has served as the head of product management at Palo Alto Networks, overseeing the product strategy and roadmap and playing a key role in delivering our Next-Generation Security Platform. In August 2025, he joined the Palo Alto Networks Board of Directors and became chief product and technology officer with responsibility for driving the company's technology vision and leading both the engineering and product organizations for the company. Prior to Palo Alto Networks, he was the director of product management for Juniper Networks, where he was responsible for firewall/VPN platforms and software. He joined Juniper Networks through the NetScreen Technologies acquisition, where he managed the same product line. Previously, he held various positions at Excite@Home and Packard Bell-NEC. He holds a bachelor's degree in engineering from Cornell University. close Nir Zuk Founder - Emeritus Nir Zuk brings a wealth of network security expertise and industry experience to Palo Alto Networks. Prior to co-founding Palo Alto Networks, Nir was CTO at NetScreen Technologies, which was acquired by Juniper Networks in 2004. Prior to NetScreen, Nir was co-founder and CTO at OneSecure, a pioneer in intrusion prevention and detection appliances. Nir was also a principal engineer at Check Point Software Technologies, and was one of the developers of stateful inspection technology. close Dipak Golechha Chief Financial Officer Dipak Golechha joined Palo Alto Networks in 2020 and serves as chief financial officer. A seasoned finance leader, prior to Palo Alto Networks, Golechha held a variety of global leadership and executive roles around the globe. During 18 years at Procter & Gamble, he served as global divisional CFO and chief operating officer. Following that, he was the CFO for Chobani and the CFO at The Nature’s Bounty Company, a $3 billion+ CPG company focused on health and wellness. Golechha also served four years as CEO of Excelligence Learning Corp, a tech-enabled education company. Golechha previously served as a board member of Holland & Barrett International, Dr Organic, and Procter & Gamble Financial Services. He holds both bachelor’s and master’s degrees in economics from St. John’s College, Cambridge University, UK. close Helmut Reisinger CEO for EMEA As CEO for Europe, the Middle East and Africa (EMEA), Helmut is responsible for all go-to-market functions across EMEA. Prior to joining Palo Alto Networks in 2022, Helmut was CEO of Orange Business Services (OBS), leading its global organization of 28,000 employees and supporting the digital transformation of enterprise customers around the world. Prior to OBS, Helmut held leadership positions across Europe at Avaya Inc., NextiraOne Germany and Alcatel Austria. Helmut holds a Ph.D. from Vienna University for Economics and Business (WU), and speaks English, French, German and Spanish. close Bruce R. Byrd Executive Vice President and General Counsel Bruce is responsible, globally, for legal affairs, policy and government affairs, corporate governance, compliance, and risk and resilience at the world’s leading cybersecurity company. He joined the company in January 2021. Before joining Palo Alto Networks, Bruce was the Chief Legal Officer of AT&T Communications, responsible for legal affairs at AT&T’s largest operating company. Prior to AT&T, Bruce was a partner at Dickinson Wright PLLC, where he maintained an active commercial litigation, regulatory and class action defense practice. Bruce is committed to community service. He is on the Board of Directors for the International Institute for Conflict Prevention and Resolution, and chairs the Bay Area Heart Walk for the American Heart Association. Bruce graduated from the University of Michigan with a degree in economics and received his J.D., cum laude, from the University of Michigan Law School. close Kelly Waldher Chief Marketing Officer Kelly Waldher is Chief Marketing Officer for Palo Alto Networks. He is responsible for the transformation and scale of the company's go-to-market initiatives, establishing Palo Alto Networks as the foremost authority in AI and cybersecurity. By leading the company’s global marketing efforts, Kelly aims to position Palo Alto Networks as the go-to platform for cutting-edge, AI-powered security solutions. Kelly is a tenured marketing executive with a diverse background spanning top tech companies. Prior to joining Palo Alto Networks, he served as the CMO at Zendesk, where he led marketing, communications and sales development teams and was the General Manager of the online business segment. As the Vice President for Google Workspace, he oversaw global marketing functions, including brand management, product marketing, demand generation, growth strategies

Meet our leadership team See All Nikesh Arora Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Read more BJ Jenkins President Read more Karim Temsamani President, Next Generation Security Read more Lee Klarich Chief Product and Technology Officer Read more Nir Zuk Founder - Emeritus Read more Dipak Golechha Chief Financial Officer Read more Helmut Reisinger CEO for EMEA Read more Bruce R. Byrd Executive Vice President and General Counsel Read more Kelly Waldher Chief Marketing Officer Read more Meerah Rajavel Chief Information Officer Read more Danielle Gonzalez Chief People Officer Read more Nikesh Arora Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Nikesh Arora is chairman and CEO of Palo Alto Networks, the world’s leading cybersecurity company. During his tenure of seven years, he has led the company through a major transformation to become the global leader in AI and cybersecurity, and the security partner of choice for enterprise organizations and governments around the world. Before joining Palo Alto Networks in 2018, Mr. Arora served as president and chief operating officer of SoftBank Group Corp. Prior to that, Mr. Arora spent ten years at Google, Inc. as a senior executive, where he was senior vice president and chief business officer, president of global sales operations and business development, and president of Europe, the Middle East and Africa. His career also includes serving as chief marketing officer for the T-Mobile International Division of Deutsche Telekom AG, and chief executive officer and founder of T-Motion PLC, which merged with T-Mobile International in 2002. Nikesh Arora serves on the board of Uber Technologies, Inc., and as a non-executive director of Compagnie Financière Richemont S.A., a public Switzerland-based luxury goods holding company. Previously, he served on the boards of SoftBank Group Corp., Airtel, Aviva, Sprint Corp., Colgate-Palmolive Inc., Tipping Point and Yahoo! Japan, among others. He holds an M.B.A. in business administration from Northeastern University, an M.S. in finance from Boston College, and a B. Tech. in electrical engineering from the Institute of Technology at Banaras Hindu University. He has received numerous awards for leadership and influence shaping the culture of Palo Alto Networks including Newsweek's Most Loved Workplaces (2023, 2022, 2021), CRN’s Top 100 Executives “Most influential”, Comparably’s Annual list of Best CEOs for Women, with a score of 100 on the Disability Equality Index (2023, 2022), and HRC Best Places for LGBTQ+ Equality (2022). close BJ Jenkins President BJ joined Palo Alto Networks in August 2021. As president, BJ oversees the company’s go-to-market functions, including sales, support, consulting, business development and operations, and partnerships. Previously, BJ served as president and CEO of Barracuda Networks, leading the company’s IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, as well as the strategic acquisitions of SignNow, Intronis, Sonian and PhishLine. Prior to joining Barracuda, BJ held multiple business unit and sales and marketing leadership roles at EMC, including president of EMC’s Backup Recovery Systems (BRS) Division. BJ earned an engineering degree from the University of Illinois and an M.B.A. from Harvard Business School. close Karim Temsamani President, Next Generation Security Karim Temsamani joined Palo Alto Networks in September 2024. As President, Next Generation Security, Karim leads GTM programs and global sales for the company's cloud security, security operations and network security platforms. Karim has spent his career building and scaling global businesses. Previously, he served as CEO of Cardlytics, a digital advertising platform widely used in the banking industry. Prior to Cardlytics, Karim led product and engineering for Stripe's banking and financial products. He also spent 12 years at Google, where he oversaw sales and operations across the AsiaPacific region and established Google's successful mobile advertising business. Karim began his career in the media and publishing industries and earned a degree in International Affairs at the European Business School Paris. close Lee Klarich Chief Product and Technology Officer Since early product inception in 2006, Lee Klarich has served as the head of product management at Palo Alto Networks, overseeing the product strategy and roadmap and playing a key role in delivering our Next-Generation Security Platform. In August 2025, he joined the Palo Alto Networks Board of Directors and became chief product and technology officer with responsibility for driving the company's technology vision and leading both the engineering and product organizations for the company. Prior to Palo Alto Networks, he was the director of product management for Juniper Networks, where he was responsible for firewall/VPN platforms and software. He joined Juniper Networks through the NetScreen Technologies acquisition, where he managed the same product line. Previously, he held various positions at Excite@Home and Packard Bell-NEC. He holds a bachelor's degree in engineering from Cornell University. close Nir Zuk Founder - Emeritus Nir Zuk brings a wealth of network security expertise and industry experience to Palo Alto Networks. Prior to co-founding Palo Alto Networks, Nir was CTO at NetScreen Technologies, which was acquired by Juniper Networks in 2004. Prior to NetScreen, Nir was co-founder and CTO at OneSecure, a pioneer in intrusion prevention and detection appliances. Nir was also a principal engineer at Check Point Software Technologies, and was one of the developers of stateful inspection technology. close Dipak Golechha Chief Financial Officer Dipak Golechha joined Palo Alto Networks in 2020 and serves as chief financial officer. A seasoned finance leader, prior to Palo Alto Networks, Golechha held a variety of global leadership and executive roles around the globe. During 18 years at Procter & Gamble, he served as global divisional CFO and chief operating officer. Following that, he was the CFO for Chobani and the CFO at The Nature’s Bounty Company, a $3 billion+ CPG company focused on health and wellness. Golechha also served four years as CEO of Excelligence Learning Corp, a tech-enabled education company. Golechha previously served as a board member of Holland & Barrett International, Dr Organic, and Procter & Gamble Financial Services. He holds both bachelor’s and master’s degrees in economics from St. John’s College, Cambridge University, UK. close Helmut Reisinger CEO for EMEA As CEO for Europe, the Middle East and Africa (EMEA), Helmut is responsible for all go-to-market functions across EMEA. Prior to joining Palo Alto Networks in 2022, Helmut was CEO of Orange Business Services (OBS), leading its global organization of 28,000 employees and supporting the digital transformation of enterprise customers around the world. Prior to OBS, Helmut held leadership positions across Europe at Avaya Inc., NextiraOne Germany and Alcatel Austria. Helmut holds a Ph.D. from Vienna University for Economics and Business (WU), and speaks English, French, German and Spanish. close Bruce R. Byrd Executive Vice President and General Counsel Bruce is responsible, globally, for legal affairs, policy and government affairs, corporate governance, compliance, and risk and resilience at the world’s leading cybersecurity company. He joined the company in January 2021. Before joining Palo Alto Networks, Bruce was the Chief Legal Officer of AT&T Communications, responsible for legal affairs at AT&T’s largest operating company. Prior to AT&T, Bruce was a partner at Dickinson Wright PLLC, where he maintained an active commercial litigation, regulatory and class action defense practice. Bruce is committed to community service. He is on the Board of Directors for the International Institute for Conflict Prevention and Resolution, and chairs the Bay Area Heart Walk for the American Heart Association. Bruce graduated from the University of Michigan with a degree in economics and received his J.D., cum laude, from the University of Michigan Law School. close Kelly Waldher Chief Marketing Officer Kelly Waldher is Chief Marketing Officer for Palo Alto Networks. He is responsible for the transformation and scale of the company's go-to-market initiatives, establishing Palo Alto Networks as the foremost authority in AI and cybersecurity. By leading the company’s global marketing efforts, Kelly aims to position Palo Alto Networks as the go-to platform for cutting-edge, AI-powered security solutions. Kelly is a tenured marketing executive with a diverse background spanning top tech companies. Prior to joining Palo Alto Networks, he served as the CMO at Zendesk, where he led marketing, communications and sales development teams and was the General Manager of the online business segment. As the Vice President for Google Workspace, he oversaw global marketing functions, including brand management, product marketing, demand generation, growth strategies

Privacy Policy | EVORA Privacy Policy Last updated: 5/10/2026 This Privacy Policy describes how EVORA ("we", "our", "us") collects, uses, and shares information about you when you use our websites, applications, products, and services (collectively, the "Services"). 1. Information We Collect We collect the following categories of information: Account & Contact Information : name, email address, handle, phone (if provided). Usage Data : interactions with the app, clicks, page views, device information, IP address. Location : approximate location for requesting mobile charging services (with your consent). Payment : processed via third-party providers; EVORA does not store full card details. 2. How We Use Information Provide and improve the Services, including dispatching mobile charging units. Communicate with you about updates, promotions, and security notices. Personalize experience and measure performance. Comply with legal obligations and prevent fraud or misuse. 3. Sharing of Information With vendors and service providers who help us operate the Services under appropriate safeguards. For legal reasons when we believe disclosure is required by applicable law, regulation, or legal process. With your consent or at your direction. 4. Cookies & Similar Technologies We use cookies, local storage, and similar technologies for authentication, preferences, analytics, and to enhance your experience. You may manage cookies in your browser settings; some features may not function without them. 5. Data Retention We retain information for as long as necessary to provide the Services, comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce agreements. 6. Your Rights Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, or restrict processing of your personal data. You can exercise these rights by contacting us at hello@evora.network . 7. Children Our Services are not directed to children under 13, and we do not knowingly collect their personal data. 8. International Transfers Your information may be transferred and processed outside your country of residence. We use appropriate safeguards where required by law. 9. Security We employ technical and organizational measures designed to protect your information. However, no system is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security. 10. Changes to this Policy We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will post the updated version with a new “Last updated” date. 11. Contact Us For questions or requests, contact hello@evora.network .

You agree to use the Services only for lawful purposes and in compliance with these Terms and applicable laws. We may modify, suspend, or discontinue the Services at any time with or without notice. 2. Accounts You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities under your account. Provide accurate and up-to-date information when creating an account or interacting with the Services. 3. Payments Payments are processed by third-party providers. By submitting a payment, you agree to their terms and privacy policies. EVORA does not store full card details. 4.
